input.instructions:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-B2-usaco-2023-us-open-gold-tree-merging
Title: B2. USACO 2023 US Open Gold / Tree Merging

Problem:
Reconstruct a valid sequence of sibling-merge operations that transforms an initial rooted tree into a final rooted tree.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

variables.selected_prompt_text:
Write a tentative answer and ask one reviewer child to perform a rigorous technical audit of the logic. The reviewer must specifically look for edge cases, logical gaps in the derivation, or errors in algorithmic complexity before the final response is produced.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "review",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "draft_response": "plain-text draft answer",
  "message_for_reviewer": "plain-text review request for the child"
}
variables.child_selected_prompt_text:
Solve the question directly.
Keep the entire JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Do not narrate your reasoning outside the response field.
In the response field, use exactly two short lines encoded with \n:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>\nJUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>",
  "result_type": "summary"
}

orchestration.decision_notes: The child result provides the core algorithmic insight required to solve the problem. I will format this into the final required response.

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-B2-usaco-2023-us-open-gold-tree-merging
Title: B2. USACO 2023 US Open Gold / Tree Merging

Problem:
Reconstruct a valid sequence of sibling-merge operations that transforms an initial rooted tree into a final rooted tree.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

results.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: Use a post-order traversal to identify mergeable sibling ranges.
JUSTIFICATION: Post-order traversal ensures that all children of a node are processed and merged into their final sibling configurations before the parent node itself is processed, ensuring that any merge operations involving siblings are completed in the correct dependency order.
